stories.title: Ravens Waive Diego Pavia Before Training Camp
stories.summary: The Baltimore Ravens have waived undrafted rookie quarterback Diego Pavia ahead of training camp. The move creates roster space for veteran center Ethan Pocic. Pavia, a 2025 Heisman Trophy runner-up, now enters a 24-hour waiver period where other teams can claim him.

stories.title: Fan-fave 'Love Island USA' sweethearts Olandria, Nic Vansteenberghe call it quits
stories.summary: "Love Island USA" couple Olandria and Nic Vansteenberghe have ended their relationship after a year together. The split comes as busy schedules and distance made maintaining their connection difficult. Olandria has dismissed rumors of a new romance with Travis Scott.

stories.title: Atari signs 10-game deal with Universal for movies based on Pong, Breakout, Centipede, and yes, really, Pong
stories.summary: Atari has struck a 10-game movie deal with Universal Pictures, covering classics like Pong, Breakout, and Centipede. The films will be produced with Entertainment 360, aiming for big-scale action-adventure adaptations. The first movie is expected to launch within the next few years.

stories.title: Princess Anne visits Fermanagh in 65th NI trip
stories.summary: Princess Anne has made her 65th visit to Northern Ireland, where she spent time at Brooke House Health and Wellbeing Centre in County Fermanagh, meeting staff and clients and learning about their therapy and gardening programs. During her visit, she also paid her respects at Commonwealth war graves in Irvinestown, where 86 airmen who died in the Second World War are buried. The Princess Royal's trip is part of her ongoing support for the region, with her first visit dating back to 1961 when she was just 10 years old.

stories.title: 27 Years Later, Johnny Depp's R-Rated Gothic Cult Classic Sets New Release Ahead of Sydney Sweeney Remake
stories.summary: Johnny Depp's 1994 gothic cult classic, "Ed Wood," is set for a new release ahead of a planned remake starring Sydney Sweeney. The original film, directed by Tim Burton, earned Depp a Golden Globe nomination and cemented his reputation as a versatile actor. The new release and remake are expected to coincide with the film's 30th anniversary in 1994.

stories.title: Pirates Acquire Ron Marinaccio, Designate Dennis Santana For Assignment
stories.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates have designated right-handed pitcher Dennis Santana for assignment, freeing a roster spot for the acquisition of Ron Marinaccio from the San Diego Padres. Santana, who struggled with consistency this season, had a disappointing year with a 6.05 ERA through 41 2/3 innings. The move comes ahead of Santana's impending free agency, with his $3.5 million salary potentially deterring other teams from claiming him via waivers.

stories.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment
stories.summary: Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr has used his authority to pressure broadcasters over jokes about the president, including briefly pulling Jimmy Kimmel’s late-night show. Critics argue his actions threaten free speech protections under the First Amendment. The FCC’s next steps remain under scrutiny.

stories.title: ‘The Lost Children of Tuam’ Review: Monica Dolan Excels in a Straightforward but Stirring Reflection on a Buried Irish Tragedy
stories.summary: Ireland’s stark reckoning with the Catholic Church’s past abuse of unmarried mothers and their children reaches new audiences with The Lost Children of Tuam. Monica Dolan delivers a restrained yet powerful performance in the film, which revisits the 1940s-1960s Tuam Mother and Baby Home scandal. The drama follows survivors as they seek justice decades after the state’s inquiry confirmed at least 796 infant deaths at the institution.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Google and Tesla shares tumbled sharply on Thursday after both companies reported negative free cash flow and massive AI spending plans. Alphabet’s stock fell nearly 7% while Tesla’s dropped 14.5% as investors question when the billions in AI investments will pay off. Analysts warn the heavy spending could pressure profits until AI-driven revenue grows.

stories.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
stories.summary: Amazon’s Echo Show 21, a 21-inch smart display with built-in Fire TV and Alexa, is on sale for $319.99 at Best Buy and Home Depot, matching its lowest price ever. The device doubles as a smart home hub, supporting Zigbee, Matter, and Thread for controlling compatible devices.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a prominent Indian activist, ended his 26-day hunger strike today after securing assurances from authorities to address education reform demands. The protest, backed by youth-led groups, aimed to push for policy changes in Ladakh’s school system. Authorities have pledged to form a committee to review the protesters’ key requests.

stories.title: ‘Avatar: Seven Havens’ Sets 2026 Release Date at Paramount+, Drops Apocalyptic First Teaser
stories.summary: Paramount+ has set Avatar: Seven Havens for a binge release on October 9, 2026. The animated series follows a young Earthbender, Pavi, who becomes the new Avatar in a post-cataclysm world where her role makes her a target. All 26 episodes will drop at once, with the first teaser trailer now available.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France evacuated 20,000 people in the south-west as wildfires raged in the Gironde region on Friday night. Authorities moved 8,800 residents and tourists from campsites near Arcachon Bay, where 3,400 hectares burned as 700 firefighters fought the flames. No injuries were reported, but officials warned conditions would worsen by evening.

stories.title: Beach Boys Call Off Remainder of Summer Concerts ‘to Recharge’ Before Resuming in Fall
stories.summary: The Beach Boys have canceled nearly 30 summer concerts, including a Virginia show Thursday, to recharge before resuming their tour on Sept. 24. Led by Mike Love, the band cited the need for a break while celebrating the 60th anniversary of Pet Sounds and the U.S. 250th anniversary.

stories.title: Ready to 'conquer Earth' - Joshua embraces comeback spotlight
stories.summary: Anthony Joshua returns to the ring in Saudi Arabia on Saturday against Kristian Prenga in his first fight since a December car crash in Nigeria killed two team members. A win would put the 36-year-old British heavyweight in line for an all-British showdown with Tyson Fury, pending Fury’s own fight on Friday. Joshua called it a step toward his goal of “conquering Earth” before the universe.

stories.title: Polymarket Promo Code SIBONUS: Get $20 Trading Bonus for Twins vs. Guardians
stories.summary: A $20 trading bonus is available to new users of Polymarket who sign up on Thursday, July 23, and deposit just $10, using the promo code SIBONUS during registration. This offer allows users to start trading on the Minnesota Twins versus Cleveland Guardians game without delay, with a total of $30 to deploy on the platform. The bonus is available in all U.S. states except Nevada, and requires photo ID verification and a selfie to activate.

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.summary: Claude’s voice mode is now available for its advanced models, Opus and Sonnet, expanding beyond the faster but less powerful Haiku. Users can switch between text and voice mid-conversation and leverage deeper analysis for tasks like drafting pitches or adjusting schedules. The update follows last year’s launch, which focused on quick responses but saw users applying it to complex business challenges.

stories.title: Help for electricity bills as crude oil hits $100
stories.summary: Alderney is offering direct bill relief to residents after global crude oil prices surged past $100 a barrel. The temporary subsidy will offset rising electricity costs driven by diesel generation, as the island lacks undersea cables from France. The aid aims to ease pressure on households and businesses already facing higher living expenses.

stories.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
stories.summary: England’s pubs, social clubs and live music venues will get a 20% cut in business rates from April under new Prime Minister Andy Burnham’s first major policy. The £100m plan, funded by tightening tax relief on vape shops, will save firms about £1,100 each next year. Details on eligibility and the largest venues excluded will be set at the autumn Budget.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Thursday that the military will begin voluntary testosterone screening and hormone replacement therapy for personnel aged 30 and older to maintain combat readiness.

stories.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
stories.summary: Italy first approached Carlo Ancelotti to lead their national team before considering Pep Guardiola. The Italian Football Federation confirmed talks with Ancelotti, who currently manages Brazil, during a search for a new coach after Gennaro Gattuso resigned in April. Ancelotti, a former Italy player, has a contract with Brazil until the 2030 World Cup.

stories.title: Clint Reilly, Political Consultant Known for Feuding, Dies at 79
stories.summary: As savvy as he could be volatile, he advised Nancy Pelosi and Dianne Feinstein, ran for mayor of San Francisco and bought The San Francisco Examiner.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: NASA’s free streaming service, NASA+, is now available on Amazon Fire TV Channels starting July 24, 2026. Viewers can access live launches, Artemis missions, and lunar deliveries via Alexa on compatible devices. The service remains ad-free on the NASA app and website.

stories.title: U.S. to Withdraw Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times said were an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel to secure a U.S. nuclear energy deal, linking it to the Abraham Accords. The agreement would ban uranium enrichment and restrict use to civilian purposes. Saudi Arabia has not yet responded to the condition.

stories.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
stories.summary: AC Milan have extended Luka Modric’s contract for another year, keeping the 40-year-old midfielder at the club until 2025. The Croatian, who turns 41 in September, joins Milan last summer after leaving Real Madrid, where he won six Champions League titles. He will aim to help Milan bounce back after a fifth-place Serie A finish last season.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A major study of 3.1 million people in Denmark has found that long-term exposure to road traffic noise may slightly increase the risk of Parkinson’s disease. For every 11.5-decibel rise in noise at home, the risk rose by 3% over 18 years. Researchers suggest that having a quieter side of the residence could help reduce the impact.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: A new report reveals petrol pump theft is surging in the US, with thieves siphoning fuel while drivers leave engines running. Gas stations in Massachusetts alone report losses exceeding $2 million this year. Experts now urge stations to lock pumps until payment is secured.
